Question: For the vector-valued function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = ( t^{2} - 3 t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + ( 4 t + 1 ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ evaluate $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( 0 ) , \mathbf{\text{r}} ( 1 ) , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{r}} ( -4 ) .$$ Does this function have any domain restrictions?
Answer: $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( 0 ) = \mathbf{\text{j}} , \mathbf{\text{r}} ( 1 ) = -2 \mathbf{\text{i}} + 5 \mathbf{\text{j}} , \mathbf{\text{r}} ( -4 ) = 28 \mathbf{\text{i}} - 15 \mathbf{\text{j}}$$ The domain of $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = ( t^{2} - 3 t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + ( 4 t + 1 ) \mathbf{\text{j}}$$ is all real numbers.

Question: Create a graph of the vector-valued function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = ( t^{2} - 1 ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + ( 2 t - 3 ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ $$0 \le t \le 3 .$$

Calculate $$\underset{t \rightarrow -2}{\text{lim}} \text{r} \left(\right. t \left.\right)$$ for the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \sqrt{t^{2} - 3 t - 1} \mathbf{\text{i}} + ( 4 t + 3 ) \mathbf{\text{j}} + \text{sin} \frac{( t + 1 ) \pi}{2} \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$
